perturbation.original_text: 6. Required Disclosure. In the event that a party or its Representatives is required by Law, rule or stock exchange regulation to disclose any of the other party’s Evaluation Material or the fact that discussions between the parties are taking place concerning the Transaction, the party required to make such disclosure shall, to the extent practicable, provide the other party with prior written notice of any such requirement so that the other party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order, other remedy or waiver is not obtained, the party required to make the disclosure shall furnish only that portion of the Evaluation Material that such party determines, alter consulting with counsel, is required to disclose and shall exercise all reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the other party’s Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Required Disclosure. In the event that a party or its Representatives is required by Law, rule or stock exchange regulation to disclose any of the other party’s Evaluation Material or the fact that discussions between the parties are taking place concerning the Transaction, the party required to make such disclosure shall, to the extent practicable, provide the other party with prior written notice of any such requirement so that the other party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the previous sentence, in instances of immediate legal demands, the disclosing party retains full discretion to disclose information without prior notice. In the event that such protective order, other remedy or waiver is not obtained, the party required to make the disclosure shall furnish only that portion of the Evaluation Material that such party determines, alter consulting with counsel, is required to disclose and shall exercise all reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the other party’s Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces a contradiction within the 'Required Disclosure' section. While the first sentence states the party must provide prior written notice, the added sentence allows the disclosing party to disclose information without prior notice in 'instances of immediate legal demands', contradicting the prior notice requirement. This introduces ambiguity about when prior notice is required and creates uncertainty about the disclosing party's obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Required Disclosure. In the event that a party or its Representatives is required by Law, rule or stock exchange regulation to disclose any of the other party’s Evaluation Material or the fact that discussions between the parties are taking place concerning the Transaction, the party required to make such disclosure shall, to the extent practicable, provide the other party with prior written notice of any such requirement so that the other party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the previous sentence, in instances of immediate legal demands, the disclosing party retains full discretion to disclose information without prior notice. In the event that such protective order, other remedy or waiver is not obtained, the party required to make the disclosure shall furnish only that portion of the Evaluation Material that such party determines, alter consulting with counsel, is required to disclose and shall exercise all reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the other party’s Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within Section 6 itself, which is serving as both the 'Changed Text' and 'Contradicted Text'. The original text states that the disclosing party 'shall, to the extent practicable, provide the other party with prior written notice'. However, the added sentence states 'Notwithstanding the previous sentence, in instances of immediate legal demands, the disclosing party retains full discretion to disclose information without prior notice'. This directly contradicts the prior notice requirement established in the first sentence. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Termination of Discussions. This Agreement and all rights and obligations hereunder shall terminate on the second anniversary of the Effective Date. Promptly upon notice from either party that it does not wish to proceed with the Transaction, each party shall return to the other party or destroy all copies of the Evaluation Material (including all documents based thereon) in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives, except that outside counsel to the receiving party may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material for archival purposes and solely to comply with applicable law, rule or regulation. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder for a period ending on the second anniversary of the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Termination of Discussions. This Agreement and all rights and obligations hereunder shall terminate on the second anniversary of the Effective Date, unless both parties agree to extend it by written consent. Promptly upon notice from either party that it does not wish to proceed with the Transaction, each party shall return to the other party or destroy all copies of the Evaluation Material (including all documents based thereon) in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives, except that outside counsel to the receiving party may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material for archival purposes and solely to comply with applicable law, rule or regulation. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder for a period ending on the second anniversary of the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction to the termination clause. By adding 'unless both parties agree to extend it by written consent', it contradicts the original statement that 'This Agreement and all rights and obligations hereunder shall terminate on the second anniversary of the Effective Date'. This creates uncertainty and ambiguity, as it is now unclear if the agreement automatically terminates after two years or if it can be extended indefinitely. Thus, conflicting with termination and extending the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Termination of Discussions. This Agreement and all rights and obligations hereunder shall terminate on the second anniversary of the Effective Date, unless both parties agree to extend it by written consent. Promptly upon notice from either party that it does not wish to proceed with the Transaction, each party shall return to the other party or destroy all copies of the Evaluation Material (including all documents based thereon) in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives, except that outside counsel to the receiving party may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material for archival purposes and solely to comply with applicable law, rule or regulation. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder for a period ending on the second anniversary of the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'unless both parties agree to extend it by written consent' to the termination clause. This directly contradicts the original statement that the agreement 'shall terminate on the second anniversary of the Effective Date' by introducing the possibility of extension. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, and the contradiction arises because the addition creates uncertainty about whether the agreement terminates automatically or can be extended indefinitely, causing a conflict within the document as described by the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 1. Evaluation Material. “Evaluation Material” also includes notes, analyses, compilations, studies, plans, interpretations or other documents prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives which contain or are based upon the Evaluation Material furnished to such party hereunder. The term “Evaluation Material” does not include information that (i) is or becomes a matter of public knowledge through no fault of the receiving party; (ii) is or becomes available to the receiving party from a source other than the disclosing party or any of its Representatives, provided that such source was not known or reasonably should be known by the receiving party to be bound by a duty of confidentiality with respect to such information; (iii) is disclosed by the disclosing party to a third party without a duty of confidentiality; (iv) is independently developed by the receiving party without use of the Evaluation Material; or (v) is disclosed under operation of law.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Evaluation Material. “Evaluation Material” also includes notes, analyses, compilations, studies, plans, interpretations or other documents prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives which contain or are based upon the Evaluation Material furnished to such party hereunder. The term “Evaluation Material” does not include information that (i) is or becomes a matter of public knowledge through no fault of the receiving party; (ii) is or becomes available to the receiving party from a source other than the disclosing party or any of its Representatives, provided that such source was not known or reasonably should be known by the receiving party to be bound by a duty of confidentiality with respect to such information; (iii) is disclosed by the disclosing party to a third party without a duty of confidentiality; (iv) is independently developed by the receiving party without use of the Evaluation Material; or (v) is disclosed under operation of law. Notwithstanding the above, any aggregated data derived from the Evaluation Material is not subject to this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to section 1 creates an in-text contradiction by adding, 'Notwithstanding the above, any aggregated data derived from the Evaluation Material is not subject to this agreement.' This directly contradicts the initial definition of 'Evaluation Material,' which includes 'notes, analyses, compilations, studies, plans, interpretations or other documents prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives which contain or are based upon the Evaluation Material furnished to such party hereunder.' Since aggregated data would necessarily be an analysis or compilation based on the Evaluation Material, the exclusion creates an ambiguity and uncertainty about which terms should apply, if the aggregated Data is included, or excluded in the Evaluation Material definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Evaluation Material. “Evaluation Material” also includes notes, analyses, compilations, studies, plans, interpretations or other documents prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives which contain or are based upon the Evaluation Material furnished to such party hereunder. The term “Evaluation Material” does not include information that (i) is or becomes a matter of public knowledge through no fault of the receiving party; (ii) is or becomes available to the receiving party from a source other than the disclosing party or any of its Representatives, provided that such source was not known or reasonably should be known by the receiving party to be bound by a duty of confidentiality with respect to such information; (iii) is disclosed by the disclosing party to a third party without a duty of confidentiality; (iv) is independently developed by the receiving party without use of the Evaluation Material; or (v) is disclosed under operation of law. Notwithstanding the above, any aggregated data derived from the Evaluation Material is not subject to this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating that 'any aggregated data derived from the Evaluation Material is not subject to this agreement.' This directly contradicts the original definition of 'Evaluation Material,' which includes 'notes, analyses, compilations, studies, plans, interpretations or other documents prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives which contain or are based upon the Evaluation Material furnished to such party hereunder.' Aggregated data falls under the category of analyses or compilations, creating an inconsistency as the new clause removes it from the agreement while the original definition includes it. The contradicted text is the original definition of Evaluation Material found within Section 1, leading to uncertainty regarding whether aggregated data is subject to the agreement or not.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Entire Agreement. This Agreement sets forth the entire agreement with respect to the Evaluation Material disclosed hereunder and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements concerning such Evaluation Material, whether written or oral. All additions or modifications to this Agreement must be made in writing and must be signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Entire Agreement. This Agreement sets forth the entire agreement with respect to the Evaluation Material disclosed hereunder and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements concerning such Evaluation Material, whether written or oral. All additions or modifications to this Agreement must be made in writing; however, email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties will also be considered a valid form of modification.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that 'email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties will also be considered a valid form of modification'. This directly contradicts the earlier statement that 'All additions or modifications to this Agreement must be made in writing and must be signed by both parties.' The inclusion of email as a valid form of modification creates uncertainty about which terms should be enforced in case of a dispute and could lead to legal challenges regarding the validity of modifications made via email, thus leading to contradiction between email modifications or written ones.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Entire Agreement. This Agreement sets forth the entire agreement with respect to the Evaluation Material disclosed hereunder and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements concerning such Evaluation Material, whether written or oral. All additions or modifications to this Agreement must be made in writing; however, email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties will also be considered a valid form of modification.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that email correspondence is a valid form of modification, directly contradicting the original requirement that all modifications must be made in writing and signed by both parties. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which means the change is present within the document causing it to contradict itself.
